heading: Protections for spouses and former spouses
label: 8435 Protections for spouses and former spouses
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. children.label: A
children.text: A married employee or Member (or former employee or Member) may withdraw all or part of a Thrift Savings Fund account under subsection (b)(2), (3), or (4) of or change a withdrawal election only if the employee or Member (or former employee or Member) satisfies the requirements of subparagraph (B). A married employee or Member (or former employee or Member) may make a withdrawal from a Thrift Savings Fund account under subsection (c)(1) of only if the employee or Member (or former employee or Member) satisfies the requirements of subparagraph (B).section 8433 of this titlesection 8433 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: An employee or Member (or former employee or Member) may make an election or change referred to in subparagraph (A) if the employee or Member and the employee’s or Member’s spouse (or the former employee or Member and the former employee’s or Member’s spouse) jointly waive, by written election, any right which the spouse may have to a survivor annuity with respect to such employee or Member (or former employee or Member) under or subsection (b).section 8434 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: that the spouse’s whereabouts cannot be determined; or

children.label: B
children.text: that, due to exceptional circumstances, requiring the spouse’s waiver would otherwise be inappropriate.

Notwithstanding any election under subsection (b) of , the method described in subsection (a)(2)(B) of such section (or, if more than one form of such method is available, the form which the Board determines to be the one which provides for a surviving spouse a survivor annuity most closely approximating the annuity of a surviving spouse under ) shall be deemed the applicable method under such subsection (b) in the case of an employee, Member, former employee, or former Member who is married on the date on which an annuity contract is purchased to provide for the employee’s, Member’s, former employee’s, or former Member’s annuity under this subchapter.section 8434 of this titlesection 8442 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a joint waiver of such method is made, in writing, by the employee or Member and the spouse; or

children.label: B
children.text: the employee or Member waives such method, in writing, after establishing to the satisfaction of the Executive Director that circumstances described under subsection (a)(2)(A) or (B) make the requirement of a joint waiver inappropriate.

An election or change of election shall not be effective under this subchapter to the extent that the election, change, or transfer conflicts with any court decree, order, or agreement described in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the decree, order, or agreement expressly relates to any portion of the balance in the employee’s or Member’s (or former employee’s or Member’s) account; and

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which payment is made, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an annuity, the date on which an annuity contract is purchased to provide for the annuity,

The Executive Director shall prescribe regulations under which this subsection shall be applied in any case in which the Executive Director receives two or more decrees, orders, or agreements referred to in paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: an election under , orsection 8434(a)(2)(E) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any court decree, order, or agreement (described in subsection (c)(2), without regard to subparagraph (B) of such subsection) which relates to such deceased individual and such former spouse,

Paragraph (1) shall apply only to payments made by the Executive Director after the date on which the Executive Director receives written notice of the election, decree, order, or agreement, and such additional information and documentation as the Executive Director may require.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the survivor annuity determined for a surviving spouse of the deceased employee, Member, former employee, or former Member under the method described in subsection (b)(1), over

children.label: B
children.text: the total amount of all other survivor annuities payable under this subchapter to other former spouses of such deceased employee, Member, former employee, or former Member based on the order of precedence provided in paragraph (4).

If more than one former spouse of a deceased employee, Member, former employee, or former Member is entitled to a survivor annuity pursuant to this subsection, the amount of each such survivor annuity shall be limited appropriately to carry out paragraph (3) in the order of precedence established for the entitlements by the chronological order of the dates on which elections are properly made pursuant to and the dates on which the court decrees, orders, or agreements applicable to the entitlement were issued, as the case may be.section 8434(a)(2)(E) of this title
Subsections (c) and (d) of shall apply to an entitlement of a former spouse to a survivor annuity under this subsection.section 8445 of this title

For the purposes of this section, a court decree, order, or agreement or an election referred to in subsection (a) of this section shall not be effective, in the case of a former spouse, to the extent that the election is inconsistent with any joint waiver previously executed with respect to such former spouse under subsection (a)(2) or (b)(2).
Any payment under this subsection to any individual bars recovery by any other individual.

children.label: A
children.text: A loan or withdrawal may be made to a married employee or Member under section 8433(g) and (h) of this title only if the employee’s or Member’s spouse consents to such loan or withdrawal in writing.

children.label: B
children.text: A consent under subparagraph (A) shall be irrevocable with respect to the loan or withdrawal to which the consent relates.

children.label: i
children.text: that the spouse’s whereabouts cannot be determined; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that, due to exceptional circumstances, requiring the employee or Member to seek the spouse’s consent would otherwise be inappropriate.
An application for a loan or withdrawal under section 8433(g) and (h) of this title shall not be approved if approval would have the result described under subsection (c)(1).

children.label: f
children.text: Waivers and notifications required by this section and waivers of the requirements for such waivers and notifications (as authorized by this section) may be made only in accordance with procedures prescribed by the Executive Director.

children.label: g
children.text: Except with respect to the making of loans or withdrawals under section 8433(g) and (h), none of the provisions of this section requiring notification to, or the consent or waiver of, a spouse or former spouse of an employee, Member, former employee, or former Member shall apply in any case in which the nonforfeitable account balance of the employee, Member, former employee, or former Member is $3,500 or less.

children.label: h
children.text: The protections provided by this section are in addition to the protections provided by .section 8467 of this title
